day_labels.news_headline: Heavy continued pressure on missile, UAV, and air-defense targets leaves the internal Phase 2 board unchanged
day_labels.news_explainer: April 5 brought continued official reporting on large-scale strikes against ballistic-missile array sites, UAV production and launching sites, and air-defense sites, while repeated Iranian missile-launch alerts through the day showed that meaningful surviving strike capacity remained.
day_labels.progress_headline: Hold day on the internal Phase 2 board
day_labels.progress_explainer: April 5 reinforced pressure on the active strike chain and on support architecture, but it did not produce a new bucket-specific breakpoint strong enough to move the internal board off the April 4 baseline.

what_changed_today.event: Large-scale strikes continued against missile, UAV, and air-defense targets
what_changed_today.description: Official reporting said the Israeli Air Force struck more than 120 targets in central and western Iran over the previous 24 hours, including ballistic-missile array sites in the IRGC, UAV production and launching sites, and air-defense sites.
what_changed_today.impact_on_model: Reinforces continued pressure on missiles, drones, and air defenses, but not enough by itself to create a fresh same-day breakpoint in any one lane.

what_changed_today.event: Repeated Iranian missile-launch alerts continued through the day
what_changed_today.description: Official reporting recorded missile-launch alerts at multiple points during the day, including 01:37, 04:08, 09:59, 13:27, and 18:09.
what_changed_today.impact_on_model: Keeps the surviving strike-capacity floor clearly meaningful and supports a hold in the missile lane rather than another increase.

what_changed_today.event: Support-architecture pressure expanded into IRGC oil-finance operations
what_changed_today.description: Official reporting described the elimination of Mohammad Reza Ashrafi Kahi, head of commerce at the IRGC's Oil Headquarters, which was described as helping fund the IRGC's ballistic-missile and UAV arrays.
what_changed_today.impact_on_model: Reinforces command and support-architecture pressure, but is not by itself strong enough to justify another same-day command increase.

what_changed_today.event: Air superiority claims remained tied to continued system dismantling
what_changed_today.description: Official reporting said dismantled air-defense systems had threatened Israeli fighter jets and that their dismantling contributed to maintaining Israeli air superiority over Iran.
what_changed_today.impact_on_model: Keeps air defenses clearly passed, but without a new air-defense-specific breakpoint large enough to move the bucket higher.

phase_2_checklist.reason_for_today_read: Sustained deep-strike access remained clearly present, and additional air-defense targets were struck, but no new air-defense-specific breakpoint justified another increase.

April 5 added continued pressure on ballistic-missile array sites, but repeated Iranian missile-launch activity through the day showed that the surviving capability floor remained meaningful enough to prevent another increase.
The day added continued strikes on UAV production and launching sites, but did not yet establish a drone-specific breakpoint strong enough to move the bucket beyond the April 4 level.

Pressure on support architecture and IRGC financing was reinforced, but the public picture did not show a new same-day break in core command-and-control continuity strong enough to justify another command increase.
April 5 affected maritime stress and regional risk, but did not provide fresh evidence of a new change in underlying Iranian naval or maritime military capability.
phase_2_checklist.interpretive_note: This bucket reflects degradation of Iranian naval and maritime military capability, not day-to-day variation in shipping disruption.

phase_2_checklist.reason_for_today_read: The public evidence did not yet establish a new hardened-regeneration breakpoint in the strike architecture.

phase_2_checklist.status_label: inside threshold band / not fully through
phase_2_checklist.reason_for_today_read: April 5 added stronger confirmation of continued pressure, but it did not become a new multi-lane gain day, so the synthetic total remains unchanged.

main_drivers: more than 120 missile, UAV, and air-defense targets struck
repeated Iranian missile-launch alerts through the day
continued pressure on IRGC support and financing architecture
no new bucket-specific breakpoint strong enough to change the internal board
summary_judgment.bottom_line: April 5 reinforced pressure across the active strike chain, but the surviving strike-capacity floor remained meaningful enough that the internal Phase 2 board stays unchanged.

next_phase_trigger_updates.trigger: The regime shifts from sustained counter-strike toward residual pressure, proxies, and internal control
next_phase_trigger_updates.daily_explainer: The regime still demonstrated meaningful direct strike capacity, so this trigger did not move beyond Partial.

next_phase_trigger_updates.trigger: Underground launch and command nodes show a declining ability to regenerate attacks
next_phase_trigger_updates.daily_explainer: April 5 reinforced ongoing pressure on the strike chain and support architecture, but did not yet establish a new public hardened-regeneration breakpoint.

next_phase_trigger_updates.trigger: Drone attacks fall to sporadic harassment rather than coordinated waves
next_phase_trigger_updates.daily_explainer: The available public evidence still did not show drone attacks collapsing into only sporadic harassment.
Maritime disruption remained strategically relevant, so this trigger did not advance.

next_phase_trigger_updates.trigger: Multiple consecutive days with no meaningful missile salvos
next_phase_trigger_updates.daily_explainer: Repeated Iranian missile-launch activity on April 5 kept this trigger unmet.
